risk_name: Safety Regulatory Compliance — ATEX, PED, Seveso III Directive

description.summary: The plant design or operation fails to meet EU safety regulations for hydrogen production and handling (ATEX 114/153, Pressure Equipment Directive 2014/68/EU, potentially Seveso III Directive 2012/18/EU). Non-compliance discovered late in construction or during commissioning requires costly redesign, delays COD, or prevents operating permit issuance.
description.detailed: Hydrogen plants must comply with: ATEX 114 (equipment) and ATEX 153 (workplace) for explosive atmosphere zoning and equipment certification. PED for pressure vessels and piping (H₂ at 30-50 bar). Seveso III if hydrogen inventory exceeds 5 tonnes (lower tier) or 50 tonnes (upper tier) — most >20 MW plants with on-site storage will be upper-tier Seveso. Compliance requires: hazardous area classification (zoning), certified equipment selection, safety case/SMS for Seveso, pressure equipment conformity assessment, and competent authority approval before operation. Multi-jurisdiction projects (different national implementations of EU directives) face additional complexity.
description.root_cause: Stringent EU industrial safety regulations. Hydrogen's specific properties (wide flammability range 4-75%, low ignition energy, embrittlement) require specialized engineering and certification. Multi-jurisdictional compliance complexity.
trigger_events: ATEX zoning study identifies discrepancies between design and regulatory requirements
PED conformity assessment body raises non-conformity during audit
Seveso safety report rejected by competent authority
Operating permit issuance delayed due to unresolved safety compliance issues

assessment.probability_rationale: Industrial gas companies (Air Liquide, Linde, Shell) have decades of experience with these regulations. New entrants face higher probability. Probability 2 (Unlikely for experienced developers, possible for new entrants).
assessment.impact_rationale: Late-stage non-compliance discovery can delay COD by 6-12 months (redesign + re-certification). €5-20M cost impact from equipment replacement, engineering rework, and delayed revenue.

preventive_actions.description: Engage notified body for PED conformity assessment and competent authority for Seveso during FEED, not after detailed design. Conduct HAZOP/HAZID with regulatory reviewer participation.
Select EPC contractor with demonstrated hydrogen or industrial gas project experience. Verify ATEX/PED/Seveso compliance track record in bid evaluation.

incident_descriptions: Holland Hydrogen I (GA-PR-003): Shell has extensive experience with Seveso and ATEX compliance from refinery operations. The HH1 safety case builds on Shell's Pernis refinery (adjacent, upper-tier Seveso). This developer experience substantially mitigates this risk.

HGHH (GA-PR-004): Kraftanlagen (BOP contractor) has industrial plant safety compliance track record. The project's moderate hydrogen inventory (compressed gas trailer loading) may keep it below upper-tier Seveso threshold, simplifying compliance.

sources.title: EU Seveso III Directive 2012/18/EU — Control of major-accident hazards involving dangerous substances
sources.author: European Commission
